title: The Fear of God Begins with His Holiness
reading:
When Isaiah saw the Lord seated on His throne, his immediate response was not excitement — it was brokenness: "Woe is me!" Why? Because genuine encounters with God's holiness expose our own sinfulness.

The fear of God grows when we recognize the vast difference between God's perfection and our imperfections. Yet God's holiness does not drive us away — it draws us toward repentance and transformation.

The burning bush, the temple vision, and the heavenly throne all reveal the same truth: God is holy beyond comprehension.
reflection:
How often do you meditate on God's holiness?

What areas of your life are exposed by His light?

How does holiness deepen worship?
abide: Holy God, help me never lose wonder at Your holiness. Purify my heart and make me more like You.
